learning_outcomes.description: Use analytical tools such as the application of Ohm's law (nodal analysis, mesh analysis, Norton and Thvenin Equivalent circuits) to analyse complex circuits containing passive components (resistors, capacitors, inductors) and dependent and independent sources.
Apply amplifier models to model the behavior of arbitrary circuits.

Apply electronic circuit models of active electronic devices such as diodes, transistors (BJTs, FETs) and operational amplifiers to analyse and model the properties and behavior of single stage amplifier circuits.

Use a systems approach to analyse and synthesise a complex multi-stage electronic circuit (i.e. the internal circuitry of a simple op-amp). In particular, students should be able to "break the circuit down" into stages; model each stage using appropriate circuit analysis techniques (both large signal and small signal behavior); And using the models for each circuit stage, predict the behavior of the entire circuit.
Apply a systems approach and frequency domain analysis techniques to predict the frequency response of amplifier circuits.

Use computerized models of electronics (including: circuit simulation package's such as CircuitLab; MATLAB) in analyzing and synthesizing electronic circuits. Students should be able to explain the limitations of such packages both in general, and in the context of a specific problem.

Apply the engineering design process to develop analogue electronic circuits which address/solve some problem (typically starting from a given set of broad, general specifications). The specifications may originate from a multi-disciplinary, real world application.
Build, test, optimize and document practical implementations of analogue electronic circuits such as multistage amplifiers.

Effectively collaborate within a group and communicate the design, development and critical analysis of an electronics design project using oral communication, written communication and appropriate engineering drawing.

assessments.due_date:
Week 2 - Week 3

Week 4 - Week 5

Week 6 - Week 7

Week 8 - Week 9

Week 10 - Week 11

During the lab/prac session.

Venue: 47-401
assessments.task_description:
Laboratory/Practical Sessions

Five lab sessions will be conducted from Week 2 during the semester. There are no regular lab sessions in Week 12 and Week 13. Marks are equally distributed among the five labs. Totalling 25 marks for this component.

Requirements:

  • Students must bring a hard copy of the lab worksheet and the breadboard.
  • If pre-lab work is assigned, it must be completed before attending the session.
  • Each session runs for 3 hours:
  • First 2 hours: complete assigned work
  • Last hour: feedback from course staff
  • All lab work is assessed individually.

Assessment:

Performance will be evaluated based on:

  • Completion of pre-lab questions
  • Active participation and practical performance
  • Completed lab worksheet with recorded results and discussion

Lab sheets will be marked, and feedback provided at the end of each session. Good preparation is essential for success.

Attendance Policy:

  • Students cannot enter the lab more than 20 minutes after the start time.
  • Student ID must be presented when submitting reports.

Academic Integrity:

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Individual hardcopy submission during the lab session.

assessments.task_description:
Tutorial Exercise 1 should be submitted by 4pm Friday, 17th April. Tutorial Exercise Submission questions will be available in the Blackboard in Week 6. Overall the submission will be worth 15 marks. 

The submissions are collected through Blackboard. The assignment problems will be a similar style and structure to those in the end of semester exam. Students are encouraged to make use of the help available during the applied classes, Ed discussion board and consultation time.

Students are encouraged to discuss the tasks with peers and teaching staff. However, copying the solutions from another student/s or any other sources is not allowed and will be considered student misconduct. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.task_description:
The final exam will be invigilated closed book and will assess all materials taught in the course. The exam duration will be 120min. More information about the exam format will be discussed during the class time. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(02 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Problem solving

The problem solving will start from the second week and will continue throughout the semester. The problem sheets will be available through Blackboard.
learning_activities.topic_description: Problem solving The problem solving will start from the second week and will continue throughout the semester. The problem sheets will be available through Blackboard.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 13(02 Mar - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Laboratory modules

The laboratory work in ELEC2400 is a series of five formal laboratory experiments consisting of (1) Diode Circuit; (2) Discrete BJT Amplifiers; (3) Multiple stage amplifiers; (4) Discrete FET Amplifiers; and (5) Instrumentation circuits. For more details please see the Blackboard course site. Make‑up sessions will be available in Week 12 and Week 13 for students with formally approved extensions.
learning_activities.topic_description: Laboratory modules The laboratory work in ELEC2400 is a series of five formal laboratory experiments consisting of (1) Diode Circuit; (2) Discrete BJT Amplifiers; (3) Multiple stage amplifiers; (4) Discrete FET Amplifiers; and (5) Instrumentation circuits. For more details please see the Blackboard course site. Make‑up sessions will be available in Week 12 and Week 13 for students with formally approved extensions.

learning_activities.topic:
Diode Circuits

Brief introduction of semiconductors, PN junction; diode circuit analysis.
learning_activities.topic_description: Diode Circuits Brief introduction of semiconductors, PN junction; diode circuit analysis.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 3 To Week 4(09 Mar - 22 Mar)
learning_activities.topic:
Bipolar Junction Transistors

Bipolar junction transistor operation in amplifier circuits; load-line technique;
large-signal equivalent circuits; bias circuits; small-signal equivalent circuits; performance of several important amplifier configurations.
learning_activities.topic_description: Bipolar Junction Transistors Bipolar junction transistor operation in amplifier circuits; load-line technique; large-signal equivalent circuits; bias circuits; small-signal equivalent circuits; performance of several important amplifier configurations.

learning_activities.topic:
Multi-stage Transistor Amplifier

Gain stage and output stage of a multistage amplifier; analyze a simplified multistage bipolar amplifier.
learning_activities.topic_description: Multi-stage Transistor Amplifier Gain stage and output stage of a multistage amplifier; analyze a simplified multistage bipolar amplifier.

learning_activities.topic:
Amplifier Models; Amplifier Applications

Basic concepts of amplifiers, Generic amplifier models, external specifications of amplifiers in selecting them for instrumentation application.
learning_activities.topic_description: Amplifier Models; Amplifier Applications Basic concepts of amplifiers, Generic amplifier models, external specifications of amplifiers in selecting them for instrumentation application.

learning_activities.topic:
Current Source

Understand the purpose of current source in the context of integrated circuits; analyze the characteristics of bipolar circuits used to build current source.
learning_activities.topic_description: Current Source Understand the purpose of current source in the context of integrated circuits; analyze the characteristics of bipolar circuits used to build current source.

learning_activities.topic:
Discrete Differential Amplifiers

Describe the characteristics and terminology of the ideal differential amplifier; conduct small signal analysis of Emitter Coupled Differential Pair
learning_activities.topic_description: Discrete Differential Amplifiers Describe the characteristics and terminology of the ideal differential amplifier; conduct small signal analysis of Emitter Coupled Differential Pair

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 9 To Week 10(27 Apr - 10 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Field Effect Transistor

Understand MOSFET operation; conduct MOSFET circuit analysis, Use the load-line technique to analyze basic FET amplifiers, Analyse bias circuits, Use small-signal equivalent circuits to analyse FET amplifiers.
learning_activities.topic_description: Field Effect Transistor Understand MOSFET operation; conduct MOSFET circuit analysis, Use the load-line technique to analyze basic FET amplifiers, Analyse bias circuits, Use small-signal equivalent circuits to analyse FET amplifiers.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 11 To Week 12(11 May - 24 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Operational Amplifier (Op-Amp)

Operational Amplifier (Op-amp) circuit analysis and applications
learning_activities.topic_description: Operational Amplifier  (Op-Amp) Operational Amplifier (Op-amp) circuit analysis and applications
